sutta: 3 the buddha said that monks the lion king of beasts in the evening comes forth from his lair having come forth from his lair he stretches himself having done so he surveys the 4 quarters in all directions having done that he utters tries his lines raw tries having uttered his lines raw he sally's fought in search of prey now monks whatsoever brute creatures hear the sound of the roaring of the lion king of beasts for the most part they are afraid they fall to quaking and trembling those that dwell in holes seek them water dwellers make for the water forest dwellers enter the forest birds mound into the air then monks whatsoever rajas elephants in village town or raja's residence are tethered with stout leather bonds such bursts and ran those bonds asunder void the excrements and in panic run to and fro thus potent monks is the lion king of beasts over brood creatures of such mighty power and majesty is he [Music] just so monks when a tattagata arises in the world an arahant a perfectly enlightened 1 perfect in knowledge and conduct welfare a world knower the unsurpassed trainer of men who can be trained teacher of devas and mankind a buddha an exalted 1 he teaches dhamma such is the being in the body such the origin of the being in the body such is the ending of the being in the body such as the practice going to end of the being in the body then monks whatsoever gave us there be long lived lovely and become happy for a long time established in lofty palaces they too on hearing the dhamma teaching of the tata gata for the most part are afraid they fall to quaking and trembling saying it seems us that we who taught ourselves permanent are after all impermanent that we who thought ourselves stable are after all unstable not to last us it seems are we and lasting we thought ourselves so it seems us that we are impermanent unstable not to last compassed with a being in the body thus potent monks is a tata gata over the world of devas and mankind of such mighty power and majesty is he and this is comparing the lion and the king of the beast with the buddha among all the animals and the lion is the chief so that even when he roars even such a huge creature that like the elephant will panic avoid his excrements and and and burst their their bonds try to run away so in the same way when the buddha teaches the dhamma there are these devas heavenly beings who have long very long lives you know millions of years and even though they live for millions of years and they never age they look like a 16 year old man or a 16 year old boy or a 16 year old girl and after millions of years they still look 16 years old so they never imagine that they're going to die and because their life is so blissful and happy and they just assume that they are not going to die but after hearing the dhamma and after observing the buddha then they realize that the buddha is 1 who speaks the truth never speaks a lie so then they realize that they are going to die 1 day and then they get scared there's some other sutas which explain when deva heavenly being is about to die and he realizes he's about to die he gets very worried thoughts run very fast very panicky he starts to sweat formerly he didn't have any body odor he starts to have body odor for me like he used to have flowers on the head naturally the flowers were built and all that so when he passes away and the body just disappears just like when he's born in the heaven the body suddenly appears it doesn't have to go through a 9-month period like human beings in the womb now the other thing that's mentioned here the being in the body is akaya as uh normally all beings we have a view that there is a self in the body we attach the body we we have attachment to this body and we take it to be the cell then as we progress along the spiritual path and then we come to a state when we realize that the body is not the cell but then we still cling to the mind as the cell and eventually we still have to let go of the mind now this is
